channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: BEARISH ON COSTCO — 111 replies — 444.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BEARISH ON COSTCO

Open thread • Replies: 111 • Updated: 2026-07-08 16:44 UTC

OP

People are scamming costcos lenient return policy now. Should we short COST?

Video:
https://x.com/Basedgoymemes/status/2073860759269077199

Early replies (first 3)

62449396
based

62449400
I wouldn't call jeets 'people'

62449441
Fuck this racist, post the person who started to do this was some white bitch, she did it for 12 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62455766
>>62453370

62455771
>>62455759
Post stack
But you won't because you're poor and you'll always be poor.

62455822
>>62455771
Rocks? Lmao what a loser

62455829
>>62455822
Sour grapes

62455840
>>62455822
You can't even afford rocks? How poor are you? Answer: extremely poor.

62455845
>>62453347
Indian immigrants are the richest because they all want to escape their country. Most other countries are livable and civilized, so rich people from other countries like to stay in their own country.

Nothing to be proud of.

62455854
>>62455845
Your country is a shithole and you hate it

62455855
>>62455759
Indian men are at the absolute bottom of the sexual pecking order in Western nations.

Projection.

62455859
>>62455854
No country is a worse shithole than India Pakistan Bangladesh. Not sure what you meant by that

62455884
>>62449441
t. brown piece of garbage kek

62455902
>>62449769
You're an invading piece of shit.
I make a point of openly yelling at one brown retard every day at least; you fuckers were born to be yelled at.
Fucking retards who can't follow the most basic rules of civilization, third world trash

62455914
>>62455859
You white incel losers hate your own country and constantly wish it is destroyed. Totally bizarre behavior

62455930
>>62455902
Cry more incel, 1000 of my relatives invade every day. Its our country niw

62455936
>>62449441
Please leave anytime.

62455942
>>62455914
>incel losers
You're an expert on the subject from your daily first hand experience

62456019
>>62455942
I would like to thank the white incel losers for letting us have your jobs, your women and your country

62456057
>>62449441

>self reporting JEET gets caught online defending his street shitting brother

Imagine the smell of that chair

62456250
>>62456057
Kek

62456293
>>62455930
Kek, we stopped letting you dirty fuckers in last year, we have enough people serving coffee and doing menial jobs already.
You faggots are all 5'6" and stink like either vile BO or a whole bottle of nigger-scented baby powder wash.
Being brown and ugly is a curse from god himself; you are marked brown like shit and can never wash it off. Ugly jeet kek

62456323
>>62449769
Your skin colour is the same as my poop
>>62451853
>jelly
The only "jelly" thing is your jamboi covered body

62456610
>>62449441
Yes this saar is correct do not be insulting India sats, India is in the bag!!

62457469
IZZATED

62457562
>>62457469
Feels good to be a winner, conquering new lands, getting rich. America is so welcoming!

62457973
>>62457562
Cope

62458144
>>62454135
They've conquered my city, not my mind. They are rude, inconsiderable, foul-smelling pricks and are insufferable to be around. They practically camp out at CostCo now. I'd rather shop at smaller, more expensive stores just to avoid their presence. There is nothing I can do about it.

62458606
>>62458144
Canadian ?

62458608
>>62458144
There is plenty you could do but you value your comfort over your country

62458622
>>62449846
LMAO

62458858
The jugaad is crazy

62460566
Jugaadjeet

item.title: Could always be worse — 83 replies — 332.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Could always be worse

Open thread • Replies: 83 • Updated: 2026-07-08 16:50 UTC

OP

You could be a German wagie.

>no more AC
>no more sick leave

These are the early signs of a collapsing country. Just wait until the food riots hit.

Early replies (first 3)

62438577
former Blackrock manager is chancellor now, imagine.

62438607
>>62438545
Do you have to send this shit to govt? What if the employer doesn’t give a shit is someone going to audit this?

62438687
>>62438607
If this is anything like Germany's neighbouring country that I live in then the doctor sends it to the government when he inputs it into the system, and then it goes to your employer automatically from there so he knows whether to honour it or not.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442433
>>62441478
Oooofff at least it's Friday.

62442461
>>62438545
That’s why I let myself be fired, collected unemployment and fucked off. Retarded country full of retarded autism max robots.

62443834
>>62440944
It would be a shame if you coughed into your hands and touched as many public surfaces as possible on the way to the doctor's.

62443931
>>62438545

Both of these are inconceivable to the Australian mind.

62444279
>>62438687
My shithole works this way too, it's ok

62444281
>>62440631
kek

62445477
>>62438545

62445676
>>62441097
what the fuck lol

62445684
the australian painter was right

62446070
>>62445477
Typical kike, gets money thrown at by frens, kikes his way through every bundesracket.

62446094
>>62438770
This problem happens especially when they are kikes(unapologetically evil, subversive and feudal), Germans(stupid, not saying it spitefully, just low IQ outside 1 area per person and zero common sense which ends up in the regulatory clusterfuck Germany and their EU is, energiewende, starting and losing WW2, etc.) or in the case of Merz, stupid AND kike.

62446167
>>62442429
Amazing arguments. Are you seriously denying all the connections AfD has to Putin’s regime? It’s the same shit with alt-right all across europe.

62446771
>>62446167
altrightfags are unironically glazing putin

62446847
>>62446771
Putin is the one good world leader

62447049
>>62438770
Kosher post.

62449791
>>62446847
100RUB ($0,0000000001) have been deposited in your account.

62449805
Merkel destroyed Germany, merz is finishing it off he will start a war with Russia he knows he cant win, the goal being the extermination of all german men just like they did to Ukraine , buy long term puts on the German market they will 1000X

62449815
>>62438545
Kek this is cucked. It's not even the corporation demanding it which makes this even worse.
>you vill work more while the rapefugees live on gibs
Kek imagine living in Germany and having to prove you're ill.

I just remembered they now have rules to prevent you from leaving for a prolonged duration despite the draft not being manadatory lol, lmao even. Oh but only males btw, females exempt.

62450529
>>62446167
I wish they had as much connections as you think. But yes they are a good thing.

62450539
>>62438770
There was no "true right wing party" in Germany that had more then 1% support. So your argument is retarded.

62450552
>>62450539
Even AFD are far leftists isn't their leader a lesbot married to a jeet?

62450633
>>62449805
Merz is an afd agent. He won't start a war with russia.
He will destroy the CDU and then hand the country with a massive military over to the afd which will proceed to invade europe

62450642
>>62450633
Is this what liberals think would happen with the afd? Lol no wonder they are scared shitless

The afd is just another puppet, nothing will change, did any of the other european countries go up in flames after their so called nazi party got into power? Heck some are even worse off with shitskins than before

62450654
>>62450633
Germany has no military

62450969
>>62438744
delusional

62454517
>>62438545
the absolute state of Goymany

62454754
>>62438545
I'd just come to work sick and spread the disease. See how that impacts the economy.

62457832
>>62454754
You cant do that!!!!!!!!!

62460540
>>62438545
At least the taxes are low right?

62460588
>cries about too much bureaucracy 24/7
>forces more bureaucracy to an already overstressed health system

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 179 replies — 272.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 179 • Updated: 2026-07-08 16:16 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62456877
>>62437938
WAGMI

62457059
BATHROOM GODS
GET FUCKING READY
THIS IS THE ENDGAME
CONGRATULATIONS TO ALL THAT MADE IT THIS FAR
ENJOY!!!!!!!!!

62457184
Will this also benefit GME?
The other thread has too many shills to get an honest answer.

62457192
>>62457184
pools closed, this is a gated community and its also whites only.
head back

62457193
>>62457184
ultimately it's not going to benefit BBBY losers or GME owners.

it's meaningless noise signifying nothing except that the BBBY BK is winding ever closer to its finish without shareholders getting a dime.

62457200
>>62457193
that's because the bathroom gods will be getting way more than a dime

62457215
>>62457200
He asked for an honest answer and I gave it

you are getting nothing

if you get something from a class action lawsuit (unlikely) it's probably going to be less than a hundred dollars total. But in reality there's a long line of actual creditors and lawyers in front of you that will take any payments that might be won.

62457221
>>62457215
we won though

62457223
>>62457221
>we won though
retard of the year award perhaps.

62457418
>>62457215
less than a hundred per share aint bad tho.

I wonder what RC & Icahn are up too?

62457938
>>62449186
What is the legal process to uncancel a stock and reopen short positions that were closed upon cancelation? I can't seem to find it in current US securities law? AI can't find it for me either and is also unable to find me any cases of this ever happening. Looks to me like this is not possible under current law.

62457968
>>62457938
It's the exact same as if you were naked short a stock that issues a dividend. The company doesn't issue more money, they shorts simply are on the hook to pay for it.
Naked shorting creates synthetic longs. Disbursements to the shareholders by the estate/corporate entity are calculated by the actual TSO. It is not the prerogative of the company to "honor" counterfeits. The shorts pay for it.

62458028
>>62457938
what your looking for is the fact that the chapter 11 plan can still legally change, and equity/cash can be distributed to previous shareholders.

then what this poster said:
>>62457968

Short positions aren't reopened because they were never closed since the company is still in active chapter 11, and when the company give $ and shares to each previous shareholder they only have to make distributions based on the number of shares issued, not the extra billions that were created by market makers as Bed Bath pushed under 10 cents.

Nice try paid stock basher, but nothing you say can argue with reason.

62458266
>>62457938
There is no plan to “uncancel” the stock. That is a strawman argument you are forced to beat into the ground.

62458308
>>62457184
GAMEshire BATHaway
>t. pulte
>t. sultan

62458310
>>62458028
>Nice try paid stock basher
I think this whole thing must be some elaborate comedy bit, or you people are just insane. Good luck though.

62458385
>>62457184
>>62458308
It's as shrimple as that

62458481
>>62458308
>>62458385
Will this lead to MOASS?

62458756
>>62458481
We will finally be free

62458765
>>62458756
Don't say stuff that makes me feel sorry for you

62459593
>>62458765
Nothing really to feel sorry for other than some lost time. We already know the shareholder interests have been preserved because we know the CUSIP is still being used. We're just waiting to see what truly took place and from there we'll get our massive tendy payout.

62459685
Imagine missing out on the single greatest victory for retail investors in the entire history of finance. Not me because I am a bathroom god.

62459707
>>62459685
It really hasn't set in yet that we're getting extremely close to the finish line here. Normally I am kind of sad about the summer days passing by. But this fall is going to be spectacular.

62459753
>>62459707
Tendie status: in the fryer
Butterfly status: emerging from the cocoon
Shill status: in shambles

62459815
>>62459753
It's all coming together nicely. And it's not even just a small little payout either. We have billionaires calling this thing Gameshire Bathaway.
It's pretty awesome being on the inside of this joke being made. That goes for all of us, Maxipads included.

62459836
>we call GME maxi pads because they are all being used to soak up the fertilizer from $GABBBYQ's aunt flo reverse swaps at the holiday inn

62459881
0€ in my account now.
From 10.000€ in 2023 and 10k share BBBYQ.

62459892
>>62458756

62459901
I'm m very disappointed.
Europoor meanwhile.

62460453
>>62459836
>we
You seem to have an issue with conflating one posters mean words for who you’re forced to pretend to be with everyone that notices your disingenuous nature.

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 116 replies — 125.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 116 • Updated: 2026-07-08 16:00 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62455526
I guess I need to see a dentist soon. That's next to do, I guess

62455565
Oh, is this the bump limit? Threads about to slide off the board?
>>62454560
You're insane or a glowie.

62455632
>>62454560
Can always start with trans feet pics. Get them all pedicured and female-passing first of course.

62455644
Are you artistically inclined?
No shit, you may be able to draw furry porn. Those guys still pay out the ass for commissions and even though AI is a thing a lot of furries refuse to use it to get their porn.

62455814
>>62449961
Good call making this on a Monday so you get the extra seethe from wagies that had to go back to work.

62455832
>>62455814
I hadn't thought of that. Lol.

62455843
>>62450003
You're practically dead already, I mean come on, can you really say you've lived at all as a NEET? And Hegseth in his infinite retardation raised the enlistment cut-off to 42 so it's an option.

62456054
>>62455832
You're not completely screwed, by the way. You'll have to work entry-level jobs for a bit to build references and show stability, though. In order of priority highest to lowest:
>phone/internet
You're posting here so I assume you have either one of these. Phone is important.
>dental
Try federally funded health centers, medicaid, dental schools, county clinics, and free clinics
>getting care for your mom
You can't even take care of yourself so try to find your mom some care. Try eldercare locator to start.
>transportation
If you're rural and don't have transportation that needs to change. The end goal here should be getting your license to drive, but if you never drove before that could take months so you'll need to find something in the meantime.
>job
If you're rural this might have to wait until you have transportation, but the goal is entry-level work to build a resume/show stability. If they ask about work history tell them you were out of the workforce for family caregiving and health reasons.

also apply for any assistance programs you can. have no shame in doing so. they're there for people in your situation

62456107
>>62449961
Your options are disability, construction or the military. Say you were caring for your mom. I'd try the military first while you are still eligible. If they declare you ineligible then either try trade school or use it as an excuse to claim disability.

62456122
I can't get an angle on my teeth to take photos, I'll have to get a dental mirror later.

62456853
>>62456122
Nope, sorry. My phone camera can't take pictures worth shit. At least nothing seems to be infected.

62457593
>>62450111

How is that a bad thing? Thats a good thing.

62457793
>>62451426
I hire and fire guys all the time. Honesty is important and if I catch a whiff of a liar in an interview its over, but in your case this anon is right. You have to lie on your resume, a 20 year gap would not make it past HR to me ever. You must research enough to be able to answer questions about what you put on there as work history so don't go too crazy. Such as explain to me in detail exactly what you did day to day at your previous made up job. Good luck.

62457849
I got into tech support without prior knowledge at 39. It's only replying to customer emails and phone calls.

Yes it doesn't pay much (40k Euros here in Germany) but it's enough for rent and an ok living. Not enough to have a car though.
The best part is that's it's depending on the company 30%-100% remote and honestly...it's easy work.
Yes A.I. will replace you in the next couple of years but there are maybe 2 years left to make a living with it.

62457884
>>62449961
You all speak about entry level jobs, starting his own business, or the (((military))).
But don't you anglo people have low level public servant exams? Class IV employee.
They are super easy, basically, to pass the exam, you need to know how to write, to count, and to have a minimum of general knowledge.
Once you pass the exam, they don't care even if you are 50. Sure the wage is minimum, and you have absolutely no way to evolve professionally. But they will not ask question. You only have to pass the exam.

62457969
>>62453510
Where did he find the clients?

62457986
>>62453537
Is the card market too inflated/saturated to bother anymore? Or do you still scalp?

62458076
>>62457793
>Honesty is important and if I catch a whiff of a liar in an interview its over, but in your case this anon is right. You have to lie on your resume, a 20 year gap would not make it past HR to me ever. You must research enough to be able to answer questions about what you put on there as work history so don't go too crazy. Such as explain to me in detail exactly what you did day to day at your previous made up job.

This is a low key admission job history doesnt matter.

62458190
>>62457884
No that doesn’t exist here. You can’t get a government job unless you’re a gay disabled Jewish black female veteran.

62458213
>>62449961
Do a trade or some type of apprenticeship through military or come to Australia and do FIFO (fly in fly out work). High pay, stack LINK. There's always hope fren

62458240
>>62450003
Depends on your country, but it's a good place to save by milking the free stuff.
Pick a civie equivalent job that isn't too physical and coast.

If you're not stupid enough to buy expensive cars and burn money on strippers you can save a lot (hookers are a more efficient use of spending money on women).
Get in, get out and collect gibs if you Jew your medical correctly

62458604
>>62449961
why do retards who ask how to get jobs never, EVER talk about their skills, experience (yes, you can be a NEET and still have experience even if you have never had a job), hobbies (that can be converted to jobs) and interests?
not asking about this particular OP, but about people in general...

62458671
You've got me searching for stuff from high school now. 20 years ago.
Jesus.

62458676
>>62458604
Because they don't think they have any skills or experience. And how would they evaluate that anyway? They have no frame of reference.

62458726
>>62458676
being ignorant about their own employable skills is exactly the reason why they should mention skills, experience, hobbies and interests.
they could simply say "I like X, Y and Z. do you think any of these things can help me get a job?". even gaymers can work as testers.


I was a NEET once upon a time, and I knew I liked computers, reading science and engineering news and stuff like that. that got me a job.

62458735
just end it bro

62458848
>>62449961
kind of fucked
your best bet if you have a car is to get a job working overnight at McDonalds or another 24 hour fast food place
if you aren't obviously a druggy or look like a psycho they will hire you on the spot, seriously
or just lie on your resume and get any menial wageslave job
just research some businesses that used to exist in your area, for example find restaurants that went out of business and say you worked as a dishwasher
if they ask about gaps in your resume you can say you were looking after a sick family member
make everything before the last 5 years or so a small business that you owned and operated yourself, for example lawnmowing

so your resume should look like this
graduated high school
worked for yourself as a believable small business like a lawnmower
"my business was destroyed by covid"
began looking after a sick relative for 2-3 years
worked as a dishwasher, or cleaning toilets, or some other thing for a business like a hotel that went out of business recently in your town or a nearby town. for 2 or so years until 6-12 months ago when that business closed
been searching for a job since then

problems with this method:
- if they check references you're fucked but no one does for these dead end jobs like McDonalds maintainence or dishwasher. there are services you can hire which pretend to be references for you but it shouldn't be necessary
- if you don't have a car you're just fucked, you need it to have a leg up on other applicants for these jobs. I'm pretty sure I was hired by McDonalds the moment I said I had my own vehicle and could be available to start at 2am
- if you get the job you're trapped in a hell dead end wage slave job

source: same situation as you OP but I was 29 rather than 39

62459478
>>62458735
Why, my life is better than yours.
>>62458848
I live miles from any McDonald's. I've got a convenience store, a place that used to be a bowling alley but turned into some other business I've never been in, OR a several mile trip along a freeway.

62460298
>>62449961
>>62449979
Some sort of low entry Zogbot job;
Coast Guard is easy
National Guard idk
Navy
Airforce

Avoid anything army related and you'll be alright.

t. Same boat but turned it around

62460390
op unironically save up enough money for a pressure washer and sign up for one of those indepdent contractor apps and start pressure washing boomers drive ways. save up enough to buy or lease a car then pivot into driving uber.


this is your only realistic path to making any kind of money beyond minimum wage

item.title: GOLD IS RUGGING MY BASTERDS — 20 replies — 80.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
GOLD IS RUGGING MY BASTERDS

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-07-08 16:52 UTC

OP

GOLD IS RUGGING MY BASTERDS

Early replies (first 3)

62459946
>>62459930
Saaar a tiger tank needs 64tobnes of godl basterd!

62459961
still overvalued

62459986
>>62459930
SHINY ROCKS

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62459993
>>62459946
Tiger Tank?

62460006
>>62459993
Saaar a v2 rocket needs 200 tonnes of silber blody! Not to mantion compoooter parts in your iphone madarchod!

62460015
BLOODY BASTERD BITCH I CALL YOUR MOTHER A GROUP

62460025
I am beheemed bloody

62460231
>>62459930
Fucking retards
>asset is rugging better sell
This is why you will never make it

62460239
>>62460231
Metalbergs never stop dishing it out but can never take it. Deal with it nerd.

62460288
>>62460231
Hope your overvalued shiny rocks keep dropping

62460311
It'll never be under 4000 again in the lifetime of a greenland shark.

62460320
>>62460231
Its just seething cryptobros down 50%+

62460324
>>62460231
>Gold goes to $2000
>"Cool, I'll sell at $3000"
>Gold goes to $3000
>"Well if it goes to $3000 maybe I'll actually sell at $4000"
>Gold goes to $4000
>"Next stop $5000!"
>Gold goes to $5000
>"Next stop $10,000!"
>Gold drops to $4000
>"BLOODY BENCHOD BINTCH DO NOT CLICK THE REDEEEEEEM BUTTON DO NOT REDEEEM THE GOLD SAAAAAARS. WE ARE THE PMG MARINES AND WE ARE NEVER SELLING"

62460378
>>62460324
Gold is unironically a jeetcoin

62460414
>>62460378
>Gold is unironically a jeetcoin
Only if you don't understand the meaning of per-capita.

62460422
>>62460311

62460442
>>62459930
>real things are getting cheaper
>benchods are panicking

62460486
>>62460239
>>62460288
>>62460320
>>62460324
>GLD 5% portfolio allocation rebalance as needed
>sell covered calls
>use cash to accumulate growth and div yield stocks
>sell covered calls on stocks
>use cash to accumulate more assets
Never sell, generate cash, accumulate stocks. /biz/ is full of actual retards.

62460556
>>62460442
The reason gold is dropping is expectations that interest rates will be higher for longer

62460594
>>62460556
This is a good thing.

item.title: Is it true that we can cure the affordability crisis and inflation if we all just buy stonks? — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is it true that we can cure the affordability crisis and inflation if we all just buy stonks?

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-07-08 16:43 UTC

OP

Is it true that we can cure the affordability crisis and inflation if we all just buy stonks?

>OK, final thought. Um, record high in the stock market. You may know that. Oil prices coming down. That was easily predictable. And they’ll continue to drop. We hope they go below $70 a barrel.

>Now, the American oil companies will take their sweet time because they want to make as much money as possible, from us, dropping at the pump, but it’ll happen. But the most important thing is that, about 40% of Americans have no stocks, no mutual funds, no IRA, nothing.

>Okay. 58% according to Gallup have stocks. This is how you grow your money in a capitalist society.
Now if you don’t have any stocks, what deduce do you think is going to happen to you when you get older? What do you think that the government is going to provide a comfortable living for you? Is that what you think?

>You’ll get Social Security, but that eeks by. You want to live in a trailer?

>Stock market is the way to amass capital. And I’m not a speculator. I just tell my guys, I have a financial advisor and you know, you have to have one by the S&P, the Standard & Poors. That means that by and just hold it. My portfolio is doing great because Trump, his policies, appeal to the big companies, but 40% of Americans don’t participate in capitalism and a lot of them are yowling about high prices?
Come on. You get 4% inflation here. Live in the real world. So you younger people, you got to get into the stock market. Don’t take big risks. Go with the conservative stuff. Final thought.

Early replies (first 3)

62459793
Its an income problem. People arent even buying fastfood as much anymore.

62459803
>>62459782
The guy you're quoting is a nepobaby coke addict.

62459807
Is it?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62459832
>>62459782
>Buy my bags saar!
No

62459839
>>62459793
>People arent even buying fastfood as much anymore.

Lmao, maybe the losers learned to cook

62459866
>>62459793
I am surprised restaurants aren't mega dumping. They're getting hit with people having less income/income not going as far and GLP-1s becoming more and more accessible.

62459872
>>62459866
Theyre all buying Sysco slop and frozen shit to cut costs.

62459879
>>62459866
Have you looked at the stocks moron

62459926
>>62459782
The cure to the affordability crisis is mass production of physical goods and services. I've already gone over this, but I have zero power and nobody gives a shit anyways. You can't tax your way out of this. You can't loan/print your way out of this. You can't "muh labor unions & eat the rich" your way out of this. And you can't "Luigi" your way out of this.

You also can't regulate your way out of this. Regulations are a major cause for all of this in the first place. They make it nearly impossible to build power plants or utility stations, they make it nearly impossible to build housing anywhere, they make it nearly impossible to experiment with new drugs, they make it nearly impossible to build new cars and they give "free" money to 18 year old tards that don't understand budgeting, while letting university administrators (95% Democrat) hike tuition fees to infinity. This is why everyone is either creating software apps on phones, crypto, or other tech related shit, because it's one of the least regulated industries.

You need mass production, which means:
- Eliminate 90% of current regulations in every industry.
- Eliminate all required hiring practices and have complete freedom of association.
- Eliminate 90% of taxes/tariffs and allow both corporations and consumers to decide where their money flows. The only things we should be funding are military, courts and direct consumer subsidies to those with actually physical and mental health handicaps, not the scams we have today.
- Serious crackdown on activist judges and radically harsher penalties for criminals.
- Privatize Social Security and turn it into a forced private savings program, like a 401k, that's only accessible at age 65+. People should control their retirement, not the government.

62459931
>>62459782
>>62459926
- Privatize Medicare & Medicaid and turn them effectively into forced HSA accounts. Allow consumers to choose any policy, from any company, at any time. End subsidies to corporations and force them to compete for their customers. Anybody that is truly impoverished and cannot fund an HSA should get a direct subsidy from the government into their account. This way they still get to choose their insurance plan and the insurance providers still need to compete for their business.
- Deport all illegal aliens.

62459940
Bill o really with some solid advice. Too bad im buying massive puts against the market

62460007
>>62459879
No.

62460013
>>62459782
>Get in the stock market
>Trump launches Iran War part 2.0
>Lose money anyways
I hate you all..

62460061
>>62460013
I boated oil today usa out of vc 2 days ago was the sign of the jew, man jews are ez nowadays.

62460067
>>62460013
Be thankful the markets already shit or youd have really gotten fucked

62460107
>>62459926
>>62459931
>Everyone and everything just needs less regulation and more privatization
How, in your mind, does that solve anything? How would privatizing social security make it better?

>you need mass production
Of what? What can we make that isn't already meeting the demand for it?
"Just make shit"? What? Tables? Hula hoops?

62460134
>>62460107
Put what I posted into Chat-GPT and tell it to steelman my argument. Ask it more questions if you're interested. AI does a fairly good job steelmanning arguments if you specifically tell it to.

>you need mass production
>Of what? What can we make that isn't already meeting the demand for it?
Housing, healthcare, energy, vehicles, literally everything people online complain about on a daily basis.

62460557
>the way you earn money isn't by providing a useful service or labor its by purchasing pieces of paper that arbitrarily rise in value because a board lied to retards about what they actually do
How do people think this shit is actually stable or even good? How retarded do you have to be to reach the conclusion that having more money to start and letting it sit somewhere and doing nothing yourself is actually the healthiest output for society? If your primary way to raise your status in society is to just throw money at the pre-existing largest companies then you've just damned all future generations into haves and have-nots off the misfortune of their birth which inherently destabilizes shit if the underclass ever gets uppity

item.title: When the FUCK is this AI shit going to crash? — 31 replies — 33.6/hr
item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-07-08 16:00 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62444975
Don’t cry Bobina

62445016
>>62444969
Oooooooooo BOOOOOOOBIIIINNNNAAAAAAAA OOOOOOOOOOOOHHHHHHHHUUUUUUUHHHHHH!!!!!!!!!!!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445048
>>62444969
55k next weeek!

62445049
bobina is thick asf

62448136
Comforting sex with Bobina

62448224
>>62444969
i unironically think it is too big to fail
>if it crashes the US economy is fucked
>elites are depending on it for their mass datacenter surveillance state
>if US companies fail then chyna wins
US government will prop it up at all costs
prove me wrong

62448245
>>62444969
Unironically soon.

62448465
>>62444969
when you FOMO in

62448519
bobina dev is a deranged individual, stay away at all costs

62449610
>>62444969
IDK enough about the finances of the companies to say, but the demand for the hardware isn't going away any time soon. If you need hardware, buy now. When M5 studio drops, it's probably going to be up to 128gb of ram, and those will sell out in a heartbeat, further driving up prices on RTX 6000's and DGX sparks when people panic-buy whatever significant compute they can.

62449618
>>62444969
that's hard to predict. in the end, Ed Zitron will be proven right.

62449622
>>62444969
Bobina best grill

62449659
>>62448519
hello chudcel

62449668
Bobos in shambles

62449893
>>62444969
H-h-help me leave my short cleanly!!

62450137
>When the FUCK is this horseless carriage shit going to crash?

This is how retarded you sound OP

62450141
>>62450137
>car: obviously useful for transportation
>llm: generates naked cartoon bears

62450151
I honestly think that the crash won’t really change shit until 5+ years after. The stock for future batches of ram and ssd are already bought up until 2029 (and will probably be gone for more batches in the future). And while slop will definitely be reduced, it will still be slop and companies will realize that ai shit isn’t good, and the job market will still be like shit (tho it won’t get much worse)

62450641
>>62444969
>spaceX going public to save X AI
>anthropic going public soon
>openai thinking about going public
>all of them spending out the ass on capex
>increasing reports from other companies that AI hasn’t been all it was cracked up to be

They’re literally going public to use us for exit liquidity. The valuations are such that the large-cap index funds will have to buy substantial stakes upon listing, and of course retail investoors will gobble it up trying to make a quick profit or thinking they’re “early.” I give it a year or so but it’s going to pop

62451288
yann wants to crash the market

62453117
>>62450141
The rule 34 bobinas that have been posted are goonworthy, but not trillion dollar market cap worthy

62453139
>>62451288
it's almost like we should have been hiring white men with degrees and shit to guide the AI or whatever, possibly even pay them middle class salaries to do so.

Oh well.

62453143
>>62450151
>until 2029!
Ram and ssd companies like Micron are already crashing.

62454757
>>62444969
2 more weeks!

62454929
>>62444969
It already is

62459075
>>62445048
Oh yes, the bearoritas.
Let me call the minotaurs real quick

62459098
>>62445048

62459295
>>62459134
Pls don't do that to bohbeaner

62460391
>>62459295

Bobos are natural crab-bucket niggers
That trash is right up their alley

item.title: Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news? — 42 replies — 32.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news?

Open thread • Replies: 42 • Updated: 2026-07-08 15:37 UTC

OP

Seems like every BTC pump or dump is blamed on ETF flows or macro headlines.

>Is that just the reality now, or are people overlooking other factors?

Early replies (first 3)

62450753
>>62450728
Its 100% cz dependent, just tradingview has to write something to the price action.

62450760
>>62450728
Btc does this thing where it lulls the newfag into a false sense of superiority. Both in bull and bear markets. In a bull market, it tricks newfags into thinking it will go up forever and traps them in overleveraged longs and in bear markets, it tricks them into it never going back up and they either sell low, sell covered calls or lever up short and get milked. Just hold spot and don't pay attention for a decade.

62450902
>>62450753
cz?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62453044
>>62450961
I'm planning to buy silver. Is it good? Silver bar maybe

62454200
>>62450753
do you think the narrative actually influences sentiment after it's published?

62454605
>>62453044
Buy btc

62454692
>>62450950
Which fucking news?

62454719
It's over

62455393
>>62454200
What does it mean when we care the price alone

62455506
>>62450728
why not buy low cap gems
>Ansem
>Dogefi

62455701
>>62454719
or the opposite

62456393
>>62450728
bitcoin will converge with the performance of a 0.5x gold etf

62457047
>>62456393
Just that? I'll for memecoins

62457075
buy the dip

62457106
>>62450728
I need honest opinion. Shall I buy more?

62457313
I'm planning to throw 10k in BTC, is it a good time?

62458651
>>62457075
You can buy the dip or stake it. It's up to you, but I'd go with staking this time.

62459192
>>62457313
Yes and 10k is nothing
>>62458651
Not sure if it's a good idea

62459225
BTC is just boring. people only invested in it because it was pumping. it has no use case outside of moving assets into another country due to different taxation regulations for crypto. the only reason it is not dumping more are instutional bagholders and MSTR. and the time frame and economy; people just got used too much to buying the dip. once certain barriers are broken, it will know no end on the way back to 4 digits.

62459233
>>62459225
No it's not boring. You're the one that's boring

62459236
>>62459233
it's pump and dump. Literally bored rn

62459265
>>62450753
Is cz a government state actor at this point? Guy seems to have a bottomless wealth

62459626
>>62459192
It's a good idea as long as you're using a protocol that supports self-custody and native staking.

62459644
>>62450961
I'm not saying sell gold buy bitcoin. I think both have a place in a portfolio. I'm just saying if you do choose to allocate to bitcoin, just be aware that it is still a volatile speculative asset that could dump 50% for no reason.

62459652
>>62459626
we learned from Celsius so it's a big issue

62459656
>>62459644
Thoughts on chainlink?

62460235
>>62459652
Celsius was centralized. Babylon's approach is different, so it's worth reading up on first.

62460240
>>62450902
You don't know who CZ is?

62460267
>>62459265
He's more than that. We don't know.

62460280
>>62451999
Amen

62460287
>>62460235
Who's using this?

62460297
>>62457106
buy btc , eth and dogefi

item.title: China Holds the Secret of Where the Oil Price Is Headed — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
China Holds the Secret of Where the Oil Price Is Headed

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-07-08 16:41 UTC

OP

>China's reduction of crude oil imports helped stop prices from reaching $200 a barrel, but it hasn't started buying again in the same amounts as before.
>If China buys oil again with gusto, prices will probably rise toward $80 a barrel, but if it doesn't, prices could stay lower, possibly in the $60-$65 range.
>China's future oil purchases could determine the oil price for years and have implications for inflation, interest rates, and the economy, with its current low imports potentially leading to billions of dollars less going to top OPEC+ producers.
https://archive.vn/JroFT

Early replies (first 3)

62454444
>>62454353
Haha sounds like the jew world order put all of its chips on china never becoming self-sufficient. Thats funny.

62454663
>>62454353
>>62454444
They just switched to Russia. And yes to other energies. I told the oil schizos the whole time. But they refused to sell high and are baggies now.

62455994
>>62454353
China's seaborne oil imports are going to drop even further in the long run. Most demand will switch to coal and electric alternatives, while domestic production and pipeline imports will cover most oil demand that remains. I would not be surprised if China imports a mere 2-3 million barrels per day by sea in 2035.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62456381
>>62454663
lots of unscheduled maintenance at russian refineries these days

62456443
>>62456381
>refineries are needed to export crude oil
Holy shit retard levels never seen here. If they would all be blown up like hohols pretent (not true) the oil exports would even rise since they cant be refined at home.

62460545
>>62456381
China import virtually no refined product from western Russia, only crude. It imports some naphtha from Russia's far East.

I thought ripple was going to give me 10K. Rugpulled again.

62457967
wow. god bless ripple and curse chainlink

62458366
>>62457923
>XRP

So. Fucking. Bullish.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62458379
>>62457923
BOOOOOOOOOM XRP. THE CURRENCY OF PATRIOTS!

62458391
>>62457956
Pr stunts like these with charities are usually some sort of tax reduction / money laundering op and the charity will pocket 99% of it
The xrp will be dumped on the market for retail tards to buy of course

62459371

62460234
>>62457923

item.title: Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/? — 153 replies — 434.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/?

Open thread • Replies: 153 • Updated: 2026-07-08 16:34 UTC

OP

Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/?

I joined the military and I wanna know how to get a million dollars. I put 10% of my income into my retirement account and the rest of my free cash into index funds.

Early replies (first 3)

62447635
>>62447595
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sZZaOR4UCnE

62447641
>>62447595
The only smart play is to aim for a 100% disability rating which you can get in ~1 to 4 years (1 enlistment).
I know several guys getting $4000+ a month for life.
I am a vet and I get nothing.

62447664
>>62447595
If you're not high rank or close to information in some other way then it's just another job. Not gonna change much in terms of how getting a million would look like, you'll have to gamble like the rest of us.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62456736
>>62456675
I flirted with the idea of Fascism for a while, but even then I was looking at it from the left-fash tradition of guys like Edmondo Rossoni. Luckily, after doing more reading on theory and history, I realized that I was right when I was younger and Marxism is the only way forward. There's a reason the Epstein class has worked so tirelessly to discredit and defeat communism, and that's because it's the anti-Epstein ideology.

62456761
>>62456736
Yeah sure buddy, you're doing so much damage to the US by doordashing to single moms in Peoria, Illinois.

62456771
>>62456761
I don't have to do anything to damage America; it is collapsing in front of our eyes under the weight of its own contradictions. Zion Don is the purest essence of America, our spiritual identity distilled into a single man, and now the whole world sees what we really are beyond all the propaganda and lies. We are the Epstein Nation.

62456794
>>62456771
Ok you think pedos run the world, but how does this help OP who is a Jr. Officer in the military invest his money?
Do you see how you've basically hijacked this thread with SNCA?

It's the opposite of advice, you are a cognitohazard.

62456800
>>62456794
Everyone knows the ruling class that controls the economy and government are bunch of pedophiles. They're criminals, and they indulge in whatever crimes they can get away with. This is an obvious fact of life, and always has been.

62456828
>>62456800
Wrong answer. Let's try again!
What is the best financial move for OP who is a Jr. Officer in the military?

62456879
>>62456828
I have no idea because I've never been in the military and never wanted to.

62456897
>>62456891
Oooohhh that's two wrong in a row. Don't worry you still have one more shot at bringing the thread back on topic.

Help answer the topic of the thread by the gracious OP:
>I joined the military and I wanna know how to get a million dollars. I put 10% of my income into my retirement account and the rest of my free cash into index funds.

Grand prize is a free night stay at the Par-A-Dice Hotel and Casino! You wouldn't want to fuck this one up, Robert!

62456913
>>62456828
That question was answered forever ago before you faggots started your Robert script (note you were nowhere near the first ITT to call out the poster's retarded false claims about how VA works).

Keep investing in the TSP. The only thing I'd add is make sure he's putting it in the "C" Fund and not the retarded default "G" Fund.

Now will you fags stop hijacking OP's thread?

62456928
>>62456897
What's the topic?

62457039
>>62456528
Seeing as how they are gang stalking Robert for warning people about joining the military I would be interested in helping pay for a lawyer if I knew more about him. Maybe ask ACUL if they want to peruse this case (unlikely). Heard from other whistle blowers they all regularly say this sort of stuff when the camera is off. It is the ones who either say it on camera or on the internet that get punished. Considering that jannies and paid shills regularly break their own rules on /k/ I believe it.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ABx4fG1ZJL4

VbMQveZu and Tgdq/+4p are glowie shills.

62457052
>>62457039
>gang stalking
Meds.

62457053
>>62457039
gang stalking isn't real. What I have are a couple of secret admirers who religiously read my twitter account and talk about me on 4chan all day.

62457076
>>62456736
Uh about that... Which brand of communism? East German with their discount Nazi uniforms? Figures since they were the most functional communists.

62457081
>>62456897
Tell him to wait for the bear market. 2030 sound good?

62457082
>>62457076
I'm a Maoist.

62457474
>>62447595
I'm a Veteran. 7 years. Officer Corps. I did not get rich but I saw a few of my peers get rich and this advice only works if you're an Officer (single Enlisted are stuck in the barracks. Sucks to suck).

1) Do not get married
2) Do not spend money on women
3) Choose an MOS that deploys FREQUENTLY (you are guaranteed to deploy more frequently than Special Forces if you go Air Defense in the Army).
3) Get a VA loan to buy a Quadplex at your first duty station.
4) VA loan can only be taken out to buy your primary residence but if you PCS, you now need a new primary residence. You've built 2-3 years of equity in the first property, which you now rent out through a property management company.

Bonus: Set up your own property management company and start managing other peoples properties, too.

5) Repeat Steps 3 and 4 until you get out of the military.

6) Go to the doctor for literally anything and everything. When you separate you will have so many service connected injuries that youll get 100% VA Disability rating ($4k tax free each month) and with that rating, free medical coverage through the VA (a massive savings in medical costs)

You can also abuse the infinite certification and education programs they offer (and you should). They say not to leave the military with less education than you had going in. Take advantage of that. If you ever find yourself in a bullshit job where you're not in the field every 3 months, and you're able to take every 4 day weekend off without hassle, use that time to build a business of some sort.

Anyone who joins the military with a plan of action in place can really turn their life around. Maybe not into millionaire status, but into a comfortable, never NEED to work again type of life.

62457579
>>62447595
the artstyle and color scheme on vanilla was so good its insane. we hit diminishing returns on polygons a long time ago

62458199
>>62457474
>2
Hookers are a military man's best friend. New Cars and divorces destroy so many finances.

Medical thing is critical, I had that taught to me and too many leave it on the table..

Deployment has to be something gay and retarded, nothing lethal

Business is hard to get into but basic money management with a budget sheet tracking your expenses are critical. The amount of shiny impulse buys add up and are easily forgotten about, hence a budget sheet

t. Military with a bs job

62458277
>>62456428
You could do the same with Manhattan too for $5,073 /mo. 36 months, not too bad. Get a hybrid course. I asked my coworker after posting this and he says he's down to going in person once a quarter

62458320
>>62457474
Wow what a fucking scam the military is

62459412
>>62458320
Fraid so

62460073
>>62458320
>>62459412
What exactly is the scam here?
Getting money and bennies for your work?
Civilian sector fags are so fucking cucked they don't get PTO, pensions, bonuses, raises, or any job security.
Not even mentioning the tax-free allowances, free travel, waived fees, 0% down home loan, 5% match on 401k, free money falling from the sky, etc.
I can only assume you guys are assmad jealous because the corporations you work for (or wish to work for) don't compensate you like this.

62460086
>>62460073
>>work
slave morality

62460104
>>62460086
I'm sure being a doordasher is much better: >>62455670

62460157
>>62460073
I'm a veteran

62460175
>>62457081
It stops at 2059. It's not a coincidence.

62460188
>>62457076
Not him. Not a bolshevik.
But USSR = Union of Soviet Socialists Republic.
Socialism =/= communism.
Even they didn't have the balls to call themselves communists.
But retarded americans always thought they were.

62460261
>>62460073
>>62460086
This. Retarded nigger-cattle / slaves / wagies believe hard work with no compensation is virtuous.
People in the 1940s would've killed everyone we tolerate today, it's nuts what rich jews succeeded in turning the greedy goyim into.
Wall Street and Silicon Valley need to be nuked

62460517
>>62460175
Samuel Benner made this chart in 1878. He didn't care about going past that. Still very generous for him to go to 180 years into the future. I think a future forecast can be made using his methods after 2059. It is just going to take some math.

item.title: AI is dying — 36 replies — 70.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI is dying

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-07-08 16:25 UTC

OP

>Over the weekend, I got a message from a tech company CEO outlining his TOTAL BAN ON AI across his organization. There are no exclusions. It is not limited to generative AI. It is not temporary. It is a full stop.
>It turns out that the corporate dream of a single “100x” human managing hordes of AI agents isn’t the optimal way to run a business

Sorry not sorry, you've been warned multiple times but kept throwing money into casino chips aka AI stocks.

https://www.inc.com/joe-procopio/the-first-company-wide-ai-ban-just-hit-my-inbox-heres-what-it-means/91357535

Early replies (first 3)

62449971
>The Ban on AI Research
>The CEO’s company had been dealing with a slump in conversion rates for the better part of 2026
>Turns out, AI-assisted research had resulted in casting a much wider net for prospects and dragged in many that were not nearly a fit. In some cases, the companies were defunct, and in one case, the company didn’t exist at all. The best the CEO could figure is that AI had just inferred it into existence

62449974
>The Ban on AI Agents
>One of the agents broke, in that was unable to scrape data it needed to generate a report. Its solution was to just generate the report anyway and then to freak out about the sudden change in what that report was saying, now that it was missing foundational information but treating it as if that entire business line had suddenly gone to zero.
>“It took a week to figure it out, because the guy who created the agent wasn’t the guy responsible for the report, and he was stalling while re-running months’ worth of reports by hand.”

62449977
>The Ban on Vibe Coding
>The reporting failure was the trigger for the ban.
>“When I discovered we had two different versions of the same module in our platform, connected to two different databases filled with two different sets of data stored in two completely different and unrelated [schemas], the next day I put the ban in place.”

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450055
>>62449963
>single “100x” human managing hordes of AI agents isn’t the optimal way to run a business

It is, but there is so little "100x" human and definitely no one is working for that specific company.

62450135
>>62450012
Speaking for myself, I do not like the idea of poors entering my country club.

62450162
>>62449963
It isn't dying, but people are waking up to the fact that it's not "Jarvis, run my company" situation and that there is a hard skill curve to coordinating agent pipelines.

62450194
>>62450012
True, but the game is already rigged for the casino either way.

62450337
Not reading all that but AI isn't going anywhere, the pace may slow down but it's not going to be binned off.

62450351
CEOniggers once again proven to be 40 IQ retards.

62450414
>>62449963
Our CEO is going all-in on AI for software development and it has boosted our throughput by 2-3x. It's not slop code, either.
We have unlimited tokens.
t. SWE at startup

62450423
>>62449963
Over the weekend my ceo told me we are adopting more ai

62452074
>>62450414
>It's not slop code
lol. LMAO even

62452087
>inc.com
What
>a tech ceo
Which company

62452090
>>62450012
>rich people investing 3 trillion into AI the last 4 years
>Nvidia a 5 trillion dollar company
>the AI market is over now, because it's over
Makes you think.

62452160
>>62450135
Exactly. There is the door so would you kindly leave?

62452209
Kek my company banned it years ago when some high level executive fed sensitive financial data to chat gpt. It qas back when chat gpt was new and novel and he was probably a boomer that didn't know what chatbots are and thought it was a super intelligence.

62452880
AI is a technology and it won't go way, it will evolve into different tools that will enhance the production. The problem is that corporations are using as an excuse to control the resources and make big data centers to get more info from you and sell it.

62452896
>>62449963
This article is a month old, fucktard.

62452909
>>62452896
Precisely, baggie.

62452925
>>62452909
continuation pattern

62452935
>>62452925
lmao

62452942
>>62452935
>you: heh my pattern is bearish
>"i disagree, I think it's bullish"
>REEEEEEEE TA SUCKS FUCK YOU

62453140
>>62452942
I never implied that. I implied you're delusional if you believe exponential growth is any sustainable long-term.

62453190
>>62453140
Probably because you are retarded.

62454009
>>62452074
All the code is written and reviewed by hand. We have custom AI agents for repetitive tasks and custom tool calls error-checking.
Works great, amazing ROI

62454042
>>62449963

There is no way I am going to ever write a regular expression or a complex SQL statement without AI EVER AGAIN.
AI solution saves time and is easy to verify.

62455423
>>62449963
its joever

62455925
>>62454009
We don’t even review our AI code anymore. Our CTO admitted the pace at which we are vibe coding quality code and feature delivery far outweighs the cons and our SWEs can keep up with helping us troubleshoot but can’t keep up with actually reviewing the code. We launched a new product and between me and one other person built the whole thing over 2,000 PRs from scratch in 3 weeks. Currently customers on it generating 6 figures in new revenue monthly, almost zero issues and when they do happen 98% of the time I just take the error throw it at Claude and it’s fixed 10m later. Crazy. I have zero coding experience btw, though I was a product manager for many years so led multiple teams of SWEs and have a clear advantage over most non coders in using AI for that and many other reasons. I keep thinking I should sell my house because I realize I’m pretty early to understanding how truly powerful this shit is and I think a lot of people are going to lose their jobs within the next few years, though I hope I’m wrong.

62460197
>>62455925
source: your ass

62460285
>>62449963
i guess i can play along with this
we bring AI underground, where it should have always been, and away from luddite normies

62460438
AI IS USED NOWHERE BUT A MODICUM OF TECH SECTOR

>The first chart below shows that so far there are no signs of profit margins rising outside the tech sector. This is ultimately what we are waiting for, because the value of AI companies today rests entirely on the promise that margins in the S&P 493 will eventually climb.

https://www.apollo.com/wealth/insights-news/insights/daily-spark/ai-the-roi-runway-could-be-long-outside-the-tech-sector

62460476
>>62452090
To be fair they just get gov bailouts if it all goes to shit so it's literally a 0 risk game for them

62460488
>>62460197
People like you are honestly so funny to me. What good does remaining ignorant do you, really? Find out the hard way, I guess. You will remember the stories like mine when you are fully replaced because nobody wants to employ a dumb twat they have no use for at all. The game hasn’t even started yet, most companies have no idea. They will all shift in time.

item.title: Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment >Property taxes >Maintenance >Repairs because something WILL… — 76 replies — 52.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment >Property taxes >Maintenance >Repairs because something WILL…

Open thread • Replies: 76 • Updated: 2026-07-08 15:28 UTC

OP

Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment
>Property taxes
>Maintenance
>Repairs because something WILL go wrong
>Even if you're "beating inflation" with a mortgage you're paying much more than its actual cost
>Closing costs
>All just so you can sell it (likely at a loss when considering inflation) so you can buy a slightly less expensive house
It's not that buying a house is a bad purchase if you can afford it, but acting like it's somehow an "investment" is absolutely retarded, you'd probably make more back from buying Pokemon cards

Early replies (first 3)

62450381
>>62450377
You're supposed to buy houses on a discount
>t. Selling for double what I purchased in 2023 after $20K in upgrades LOL

62450391
>>62450377
Idk I just want one to live in, but maybe they rent it that way person who rents pays bills and when he break something you can tell him to pay for it and then when they don't give a fuck anymore they sell it

62450397
>cbf moving every year because the landlord decides they a wankers and want to sell to some Chinese immigrants who took our jobs

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62457226
>>62457177
the top 0.000000001% do not own residential properties.

62457260
>>62457179
It's like doing the daily crossword. I come on 4chan and look for retarded ideas. They're easy to find here, almost the entire site is retardation. Then I try to identify the exact problems with the reasoning behind the ideas, and find a succinct way to disprove it. I'll usually post this disproof because it drives 4chan nuts when people disagree with their stupidity. They spout more nonsense in defense of their earlier nonsense and I get to analyze and diagnose that shit as well. Perhaps respond to it if it's particularly stupid.

it's a hobby. Keeps my critical thinking skills sharp and reminds me that most humans are dumber than children.

62457295
>>62457260
but anon, they're being retarded on purpose. You're losing

62457303
>>62457295
their motives have nothing to do with my hobby
and from what I've seen almost nobody here is pretending to be retarded. They might think they're pretending, but they're not.

>You're losing
there's nothing to win or lose

62458442
>>62450446
>It’s hard to lose money from owning a house.
Nigga what? You lose opportunity cost of down payment, you lose maintenance, you lose insurance, you lose interest payments, you lose opportunity cost of moving for better jobs. Houses you live in are never an asset.

62458496
>>62450377
Because its good for
>Banks
>Financial advisors
>Accountants
>Lawyers
>Real Estate agents
>Construction industry
>Furniture industry
But if you stop and actually think about it for yourself, its fucking stupid. Rent and actual assets is way more liberating and wealth creating.

62458570
>>62450377
Mortgages are the most accessible form of leverage known to and accepted by people. Here at least you can have zero downpayment, borrow 500k and 'gamble' it into RE. If it pays off, that is fucking sweet.

62458626
>>62458496
Rentoid cope

62458923
>>62458626
Housecuck rage because your suburbian house in ohio is depreciating while the equityGOD's portfolio is outpacing your wealth by 3 times

62458933
>>62450377
>Property taxes
Rentoids still pay them, just indirectly

62459040
>>62457226
You mean Elon musk?

62459092
>>62450377
That's the thing, it's not ment to be an investment, but every boomer and their accountant said:
"Buy an investment property, passive income, rent comes in regularly, sell it when you retire for fun money"
Then every government fed into this so they didn't have to pay social security, and now we've ended up here.

62459180
>>62450381
>t. Selling for double what I purchased in 2023 after $20K in upgrades LOL
you left out the part where you're lateraling into another plywood box that also doubled in price since 2023 with $20k of upgrades.

62459189
>>62456628
What does this even mean? Lol

62459200
>>62459180
He can't even afford that, he's priced out of the shitboxes he flipped and is moving back in with mommy and daddy. Just like the other rentoids he's basically hoping for a crash.

62459207
>>62450377
You're supposed to live your life you fucking retard
Marry and reproduce

62459227
>>62458442
>you lose maintenance
How much does that cost?
>you lose insurance
Optional if you own the home. Even then, I paid $1700 last year for insurance which is nothing. That’s easily 1 months rent by itself.
>job opportunities
Non issue
>houses you live in are never an asset
I can sell my house for more than I paid. You can’t sell the apartment that you dumped 5-6 figures into over the years. The choice is obvious

62459229
>>62459207
marriage and children is the death of the individual.

62459231
>>62459229

And ?

62459234
>>62459229
Ew fuck off you selfish retard

62459240
>>62459229
Reddit moment. You gonna quote Nietzsche next?

62459243
>>62459234
>>62459240
shouldnt you be getting your wife's son ready for the day?

62459270
>>62459262
Based

62459719
>>62459227
>How much does that cost?
Typically 1-3% of the home value annually is the expectation on average but it seems more spaced out than that. I.e. roof goes bad 16k bill one year, next year maybe minimal expense basic maintenance items like filters, or an AC servicing for a few hundred.

>Optional if you own the home. Even then, I paid $1700 last year for insurance which is nothing. That’s easily 1 months rent by itself.
If you think home insurance is optional then lmao, I would never have a 300k+ possession that could be wiped out by some other idiots stupidity or natural disaster but you do you. Where I love its not too bad but certain states or areas have very high insurance costs (FL, CO, CA, etc.) Worth considering.

>Non issue
What if you get laid off? What if you get a job offer for 60% higher salary but it requires you to move 3 hours away?

>I can sell my house for more than I paid. You can’t sell the apartment that you dumped 5-6 figures into over the years. The choice is obvious
I can sell my shares of VOO too. It depends on where you live and if you inflation adjust property values. For example, where i live property has increased by 3.7% since 2023, that sounds pretty good until you realize that cumulative inflation was 9.84% over the same duration meaning you lost money relative to inflation in my region as a home owner. You also don't get to sell the interest the bank made off you during ownership, that piece of your mortgage payment (around 80%-85% for the first 5 years) is exactly like rent, poof. You also dont get back the real-estate transaction fees, realtor fee, and transfer taxes that is usually around 8-15k for a 300k house just gone. Homeowners also dump 5-6 figures into keeping their homes just in taxes usually. Let's be honest, owning a home is a nice thing but it costs you money and is not an asset, it is a necessity that you live somewhere but there are more efficient ways to use your capital if you want to be actually wealthy.

62460098
>>62459719
>$16k for a new roof
Who the fuck is paying $16k to have their roof reshingled? I paid $4500 years ago and I only did that because the old shingles were at least 20 years old and discolored. Other “maintenance and repair” expenses are minimal in the grand scheme of things.

>If you think home insurance is optional then lmao
It is… There is no law demanding a home owner, who owns a house that’s paid off (which mine is), to have insurance. The only reason I have it is because paying ~$1700ish a year is pocket change. I live in FL btw.

>what if…..
I’m not even going to bother. We can spend all day going back and forth playing this game.

62460105
>>62460098
I see anon prefers roofs with 5 year lifespans instead of 20.

62460136
>>62455984
I was going to say the same, got mine at 362k with 3.25% and I'm sitting on a gold mine. House already gained 150,000 in equity in 6 years.

62460152
>>62450377
It lets me fuck women behind door which is quite a good investment for my cock and balls and gives me the reasurement I'm not giving my money to some boomer faggot in exchange of nothing which is again a good investment for my soul.

62460218
Its a good investment if you rent it out and can make back what you spent on it, it becomes free-ish passive income, that said being a good landlord is a skill and shit ones cant break even, you are not some globohomo LLC who can take a total loss if you let in some nigger tenants

Its also a good investment if you are renting, because renting is literally throwing money into the trash, whore yourself our or sell crack but for the love of god find a way to get enough for a downpayment, every month of rent is a month of your life wasted

if you already have something or manipulated family members to stay with them rent free, real estate is a shit investment

62460250
>>62460098
Kekalek, do whatever you want, idc. Just because the math made sense in 2021 doesn't mean it does now. I just did the amortization and cost estimates with annual rent increases for 5 and 10 year costs, remove whatever you want to tilt the scales in your favor but right now new homeowners always lose. This math is for suburban midwest at current interest rates, my finding is new buyers get annihilated buying a house right now here so why buy one?

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 119 replies — 44.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 119 • Updated: 2026-07-08 14:16 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442655
>>62439758
never trust centralised services

62442658
buy btc goys

62442668
>>62442658
oy vey

62443383
>>62442650
Well, that's good for you.
>>62442655
Same. Self-custody first, then I check if the protocol has had multiple audits.

62443384
>>62442658
Do it man!

62443930
>>62442668
Goy

62444179
I'm waiting for October, but if it goes below 55k I'll probably start slurping. Everyone who says crypto I'd dead is retarded. Not ripping = dead is their logic.

62444587
>>62426537
That's never going to happen, max will be around 45K. My plan is to DCA, I got in around 58K, stake a few on babylon, I'm waiting to see if there will be more dump

62444608
>>62444587
You really trust the Babylon protocol? It's my first time looking into it and I see I could earn 0.02+ BTC every month. $1200+ every month sounds much better than sitting in my Ledger.

62444998
>>62444608
really

62445005
>>62426512
I'm selling at 100k. I've had enough of holding and should have sold last year. I wanna get out of this shit, it's too much stress.

62445018
>>62427357
do not pull

62445689
>>62444998
DYOR

62446816
>>62445018
Who's plebs rn?

62447133
>>62445005
regrets?

62447140
Ngmi

62447522
>>62442255
Just keep going

62448616
>>62426537
this is probably correct : (

trump and the political class already made their money off crypto last couple years

62448797
>>62427359
Surely the bottom would be zero, no?

62449904
>>62448797
people like to imagine when theyre on drugs

62450375
Why

62450895
Buy Bitcoin

62450912
>>62426512
Buy mote bitcoin and wait for next halving to dump on the normies.

62453141
>>62444608
The idea is to make BTC work even while holding just like ETH instead of leaving it idle, and I like the idea of self custody on that protocol

62454511
>>62426638
this but unironically

62454976
I sold at 115k so no more plans for me.

62455432
>>62454976
Ok

62455827
>>62426535
How you doing now, bobo?

62459212
>>62455827
Lmao

62459912
>>62444587
I like the idea behind their TBV, as it helps to keep all transactions on the BTC chain
